title: Accuracy
description: Closeness of agreement between a measured quantity value and a true quantity value of a measurand.

definitions.definition: Closeness of agreement between a measured quantity value and a true quantity value of a measurand.
definitions.notes: - In essence, Accuracy reflects how close measurements are to the accepted Value, while precision gauges the reliability and consistency of those measurements.

definitions.definition: Closeness of agreement between a measured quantity value and a true quantity value of a measurand.
definitions.sources:
- VIM [ [RD18](https://www.bipm.org/en/publications/guides/vim.html) ], GUM [ [RD4](https://www.bipm.org/utils/common/documents/jcgm/JCGM_100_2008_E.pdf) ]
- Definitions used in other communities
- ACIX community
- [Community, not standard, definition]
- Used in the atmospheric correction community to represent the average difference between the modelled and measured quantities
